title: Friday Sermon 9 April 1999
description:
Forbidden to you is carrion and blood and the flesh of swine and that on which is invoked the name of anyone other than Allah and that which is strangled and that which is beaten to death and that killed by a fall and that which is gored to death and that of which a wild animal has eaten except that which you have properly slaughtered and that which has been slaughtered at an altar.  That you seek to know your share by divining arrows: a heinous practice - Today the disbelievers lost all hope that you will give up your religion so fear them not but fear Me.  This day I have perfected your religion for you and completed My blessing upon you and have chosen for you Islam as religion.  But whoso is forced by hunger without being wilfully inclined to wrong, then surely Allah is Most Forgiving, Merciful.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Maidah [The Banquet]: 3).

Where it mentions the disappointment of the disbelievers, two aspects should be kept in mind – Firstly, their efforts to bring changes to the perfect religion by arguments failed completely.  Secondly, having failed in arguments, they began to use force and left no stone unturned in scheming for its destruction.  That is why Allah says that they should not be feared; only Allah should be feared.  The more you fear Allah, the more you will become free of fear of the enemies.

Regarding the perfect religion, it was the perfection of Sharia.  The Quran has not ignored even the smallest aspects.  The favour is Prophethood and its blessings.  This favour was maximised upon the Holy Prophet.  He has distributed this favour to the whole world and we can receive its bounties even today.

Once a Jew said to Hazrat Umar that if the verse ‘This day I have perfected your religion for you …’  had been given to the Jews, they would commemorate that day as the day of Eid.  Hazrat Umar replied ‘We know the day and the place where this verse was revealed.  It was Friday and the Holy Prophet was standing in Arafat.*

The reply from Hazrat Umar explained to him that Friday is also like Eid for us and the day of Arafat was also the day of Eid.  So we enjoy a double Eid:

 It is narrated on the authority of Abbas bin Abdul Muttalib that he heard the Messenger of  Allah saying: He who is content with Allah as His Lord, with Islam as his code of life and  with Muhammad ﷺ as his Prophet has discovered the relish of faith.** 

To consider Allah as Lord means that he considers only Allah to be the sole Provider of the universe, for our physical needs we look only to Allah and no one else.  For our spiritual needs, we look to the religion of Islam.  No former religions were able to fulfil all of one’s spiritual needs, since they were incomplete and imperfect like Islam is:

The Promised Messiah says that ‘This day I have perfected your religion for you and completed My blessing upon you and have chosen for you Islam as religion’ is about the present age.***  The bounty which was perfected 1400 years ago and the religion which was perfected at that time gives us the task of spreading it to the corners of the earth.  There are great difficulties in this path but those who grasp onto Allah will not lack the resources.  Ahmadiyyat has been planted by Allah and it flourishes in the face of opposition.  This is a great sign of the truth of Ahmadiyyat.  Its being from Allah is testified by the progress it makes in the face of all worldly schemes against it.

The Jamaat should pay heed to the admonitions of the Promised Messiah.

* Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari.  'Sahih al-Bukhari'.
** Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj.  'Sahih Muslim'.
*** Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  Address on the second day of the Jalsa Salana, 27th December 1901.  'Malfuzat: ii'.  513.
